TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.150 on LAPTOP-NK9LD6E3,HHCA-Maritza,DESKTOP-RGHKNDN,LAPTOP-H0LRLV42
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following servers on is old version V0.14.146. Connected to the machines (LAPTOP-NK9LD6E3,HHCA-Maritza,DESKTOP-RGHKNDN,LAPTOP-H0LRLV42) from manage engine, download and installed the latest version V0.14.150. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Email address whitelist
TimeEntry.Notes: User has requested to whitelist 2 email address performed it

TimeEntry.Title: LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6,LAPTOP-NK9LD6E3 : Syncing issue with umbrella, update with latest version
TimeEntry.Notes: I removed the old version and successfully installed the latest version (Cisco Secure Client for Windows 5.1.16.194) on both machines. They are now syncing properly with the server. I also scanned patch at myrmm and that also updated with latest software updates.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Lightening step- Amara & Montserrat
TimeEntry.Notes: Can you check why Amara & Montserrat from our team are unable to log in.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Teams Issues
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Note:

Received a request from Buckley regarding an issue with Teams where users' faces were appearing very dark during meetings. Initially, we discussed the issue over remote chat and started troubleshooting.

Actions Taken:

  1. Teams Settings Check:
    • First, I checked and updated the Teams app on Buckley's system, ensuring that everything was up to date.
    • I also adjusted the camera settings for Teams, enabling features such as auto-adjust brightness and soft focus to enhance the video quality.
    • Teams Settings: Adjusted the settings for video brightness and video effects, making sure they were configured properly.
  2. Office Update:
    • Ensured Office 365 was up to date to avoid any possible issues caused by outdated software.
    • Checked and verified Microsoft Teams was linked correctly with the Office 365 version on the system.
  3. SFC Scan:
    • Ran the System File Checker (SFC) scan to repair any corrupted system files that could be affecting Teams or other applications.
  4. Camera Adjustments:
    • I checked the system camera settings to ensure that brightness and white balance were properly adjusted.
    • I specifically increased white balance and gamma settings to improve the visibility of faces during Teams meetings.
  5. Restart:
    • After completing the above troubleshooting steps, I restarted the system to apply the changes properly.

Conclusion:
After following these troubleshooting steps, the Teams issue related to video quality has been resolved. The dark face issue is no longer present, and the user is able to use Teams without further problems.

I updated Buckley via email and remote chat to confirm the issue was fixed. The case is now under observation until confirmation from the user, as further feedback may be required.

TimeEntry.Title: Screenconnect Latest version (V 26.1.25.9592 ) updated on machine DESKTOP-RGHKNDN,HHCA-MARITZA
TimeEntry.Notes: Checked and found that the computers machine DESKTOP-RGHKNDN,HHCA-MARITZA  were running an old version of ScreenConnect. update the software from the portal to the new version V 26.1.25.9592. After updating, verify that the version on the portal has been successfully updated to the latest version. Hence Closing this ticket .

TimeEntry.Title: MyRemote Version Update and Machine LAPTOP-H0LRLV42 LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6 JDH-HQ-PC-01
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Machines Added:
    • LAPTOP-H0LRLV42
    • LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6
    • JDH-HQ-PC-01
  • The MyRemote version 26.1.25.9592 has been updated on these machines.

All updates were successful, and the machines are now synced properly with MyRemote. Please confirm if everything is working fine. Let us know if you need any further assistance.

TimeEntry.Title: Device Non-Compliant Due to Missing Patches HHCA-Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes:
Device HHCA-Maritza was identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.
Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on the system. Proceeded with patch installation for the following updates:


113145 2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows 11, version 25H2 for x64-based Systems (KB5086672) (26200.8117)
1319951 Raptor Lake PCH CNVi WiFi[PCI\\VEN_8086&DEV_51F1] (24.20.0.4)


After installation, performed a manual patch scan to verify successful deployment and to clear the pending patch status.
Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. The system is now compliant as per patch management policy.

TimeEntry.Title: Brandi: Opened Virus Email
TimeEntry.Notes:
• Accessed the user’s machine and deleted unnecessary/flagged emails to free up space and reduce potential issues.

  • Initiated a deep system scan to check for threats and overall system health.

  • The scan is currently in progress.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Brandi: Opened Virus Email
TimeEntry.Notes: Received handover from Suresh regarding running a scan on Brandi’s system. Checked the scan status—scan has been completed and no suspicious files were found. The same has been communicated to Brandi via email. Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Scan machine
TimeEntry.Notes: Buckly opened a email, so scan from the crowdstrike and defender. machine was space...

TimeEntry.Title: LAPTOP-MTFA4MM6 : Found sync issues due to old version of Myremote,need to update
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed the machines and successfully deployed the new version of MyRemote 26.1.25.9592.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: CrowdStrike Sensor Updated with Version 7.35.20709.0: HHCA-MARITZA, DESKTOP-RGHKNDN
TimeEntry.Notes: Login into the CrowdStrike portal, we verified that the CrowdStrike Sensor Version on the following machines. Connected to the machines HHCA-MARITZA, DESKTOP-RGHKNDN from manage engine, download and installed the latest Sensor Version 7.35.20709.0. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Missing patch update on the HHCA-Maritza
TimeEntry.Notes:
Device HHCA-Maritza was identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on the system. Proceeded with patch remediation by installing the following updates:

  • Microsoft WebView2 Runtime (x64) – Version 147.0.3912.72
  • Microsoft Edge for Chromium Business (x64) – Version 147.0.3912.72

After installation, performed a manual patch scan to verify successful deployment and to clear the non-compliant status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. The system is now compliant as per patch management policy.
